authors: Katherine Kountz Mor Yachin Carol K. Winkler Dror Walter Anthony F. Lemieux
raw_title: Resistance is Far-Right from Futile: Deplatforming, Resilience, and Persistent Presence Across Platforms as Drivers of Accelerationist Politics
title_en: Resistance is Far-Right from Futile: Deplatforming, Resilience, and Persistent Presence Across Platforms as Drivers of Accelerationist Politics
title_zh: Resistance is Far-Right from Futile: Deplatforming, Resilience, and Persistent Presence Across Platforms as Drivers of Accelerationist Politics
plain_summary_en: This paper studies Resistance is Far-Right from Futile: Deplatforming, Resilience, and Persistent Presence Across Platforms as Drivers of Accelerationist Politics. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Politics, Resistance (ecology), Public opinion, Political communication. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
plain_summary_zh: This paper studies Resistance is Far-Right from Futile: Deplatforming, Resilience, and Persistent Presence Across Platforms as Drivers of Accelerationist Politics. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Politics, Resistance (ecology), Public opinion, Political communication. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Politics, Resistance (ecology), Public opinion, Political communication, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
research_direction_zh: The paper points to an active research direction around Politics, Resistance (ecology), Public opinion, Political communication, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
